text: 3 5 mm pores were placed in 5th interspace and 12 mm pores in 7th interspace.
Procedure was conducted under 8 mm of mercury of carboxynumatoric.
Dissection was started by opening mediastinal pleura along SVC and azagus vein and paying specific attention to integrity of the phrenic nerve.
Soft tissue bands were divided, mobilizing fat pad of the lateral wall of the trachea.
An imminent artillery was retracted and protected by a fan retractor
At this point, lesion was visible at the tip of soft tissue pocket right underneath the subclavian artery.
Dissection continued, dividing soft tissue bands mobilizing lesion from underneath the innominate and subclavian arteries.
Clips were used as necessary for preventive hemostasis.
Integrity of thoracic outlet structures was assured by protecting active blade away from the structures.
At this point, lesion was nearly completely mobilized from underneath of the subclavian artery and clearly visible in the wound.
During the posterior dissection, integrity of the vagus nerve was specifically assured.
Also, lymph nodes are clearly visible in the specimen.
Specimen was retrieved in the endocatch bag.
Hemostasis was assured with application of Fibrillar, which was later retrieved.
Also, Evacel was applied for further hemostasis.
Standard 28-Rent chest tube was used for drainage.
